title: Boat rental in Phu Quoc: a 2026 budget guide to costs and tips
html:
Phu Quoc, Vietnam's largest island, offers budget boat rentals from Duong Dong harbour with half-day trips from around €55 per person. BoatHire24 connects you with verified operators for affordable day charters in Phu Quoc.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Phu Quoc in 2026?

Prices for boat rentals range from €25 for a shared tour to over €800 for a private yacht, depending on size and duration. For budget travellers, group speedboat tours to the An Thoi archipelago cost around €25, 35 per person. A private 8-metre motorboat for half a day starts at approximately €180, while a full-day catamaran for up to 12 guests typically costs €500, 700. Crewed luxury yachts, such as the 26m Maiora, can exceed €2,000 per day. You can day charters in Phu Quoc for your chosen dates.

  • Shared tour to An Thoi islands: €25, 35 per person
  • Private motorboat (half-day, up to 8 guests): from €180
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 12 guests): €500, 700
  • Crewed yacht full-day (up to 20 guests): from €800

Where to set off in Phu Quoc?

Phu Quoc has several natural departure points. Duong Dong harbour is the main port for day charters and fishing boats. An Thoi, on the southern tip, provides access to the An Thoi archipelago and its 15 islands. For a quieter start, consider Ong Lang beach or Vung Bau bay in the north. Ham Ninh fishing village, on the east coast, is ideal for traditional wooden boat rentals.

Which boat to rent?

  1. Shared speedboat to An Thoi, best for solo travellers or couples, up to 18 guests, from €25 per person
  2. Private motorboat (e.g. Axopar 28), ideal for small groups, up to 8 guests, from €180 for half day; see the Limpopo charter page for rates
  3. Sailing catamaran (Lagoon 42), perfect for families, up to 12 guests, from €500 full day
  4. Luxury yacht (Maiora 26m), for special occasions, up to 20 guests, from €2,000 per day

When to go and do you need a licence?

The best season is November to March when the northeast monsoon brings calm seas and clear skies. The south-west monsoon from June to September brings rain and stronger winds. Phu Quoc's waters remain relatively sheltered, however, so year-round rentals are possible.

No licence is required in Vietnam for bareboat rentals under 15 metres, but the operator will confirm exact requirements. Most boat rentals include a captain, so you can relax and enjoy the scenery.

For the best snorkelling, head to the An Thoi archipelago in February when the water visibility often exceeds 15 nautical miles.

At a glance

  • Price band: €25, €2,000+ per day
  • Peak months: November to March
  • Trip length: half-day (4 hours) to full-day (8 hours)
  • Capacity: 2 to 20 guests
  • Currency: EUR quoted, cash or card accepted locally

Questions about boat rental in Phu Quoc

Can I rent a boat in Phu Quoc without a captain?

Bareboat rental is rare in Phu Quoc; most operators include a skipper in the price, which is safer given local navigation conditions.

What is included in the rental price?

Typically, the price covers the boat, captain, fuel, and basic insurance; food and drinks may be extra or included depending on the listing.

How do I book a budget boat in Phu Quoc?

Use BoatHire24 to compare verified listings, read reviews, and book directly with operators, ensuring no hidden fees.

Whether you choose a shared speedboat or a private catamaran, renting a boat in Phu Quoc is an affordable way to explore the archipelago. If you own a boat, you can list it on the BoatHire24 marketplace and earn income from travellers in 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Phu Quoc and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
